body_txt: Say Bob offers Alice a little gift or agrees to do Alice a favor, but Alice asks for more gifts or favors from Bob, or someone else, knowing she is likely to get it. The reasons for this usually fall under varying degrees of gluttony, Greed and It's All About Me . Quite often those receiving these extra demands will feel obligated to fulfill them anyway, especially if these characters are Extreme Doormats , even when they feel like they are being taken for granted. Usually, this is portrayed as being a very Jerkass thing to do. Compare Moving the Goalposts , The Prima Donna , Choosy Beggar , Never Lend to a Friend , and All Take and No Give (a variation of this with relationships). The Farmer and the Viper is a more extreme and outright villainous variant. If the person being taken advantage of is in a caregiving position, this may lead to Caregiver Angst from being obligated to do so much at their own expense. Overlaps with Playing the Family Card if the one doing this uses family ties to guilt-trip someone into being exploited. An Overly Generous Fool is a magnet for these types of characters. A Fake Charity is a scam that preys on people’s generous impulse. See also Exploiting Another's Wealth , for when someone justifies mooching or robbing a rich person because they can afford it. noreallife
